---
title: "nba_challenges"
output: html_document
date: "2024-09-21"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document.

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(openxlsx)
```
```{r}
# Creating a dataframe from the large list
df <- bind_rows(plst)
```

```{r}
# Removing duplicates (all around the end of quarter/start of quarter)

df <- df %>% distinct(gt, m, line, .keep_all = TRUE) #keeps last duplicate (start of next quarter)
```

```{r}
df_enhanced <- df %>%
  # Create the lagged versions of H_P (2-point and 3-point shifts) using left_join
  left_join(
    df %>% select(gt, line, m, H_P) %>% mutate(m = m + 2),  # 2-point lag
    by = c("gt", "line", "m"), #Match on gt, line, and m (shifted)
    suffix = c("", "M2") # Give the joined H_P column a new name
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    df %>% select(gt, line, m, H_P) %>% mutate(m = m + 3),  # 3-point lag
    by = c("gt", "line", "m"),
    suffix = c("", "M3")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    df %>% select(gt, line, m, H_P) %>% mutate(m = m + 4),  # 4-point lag
    by = c("gt", "line", "m"),
    suffix = c("", "M4")
  ) %>%
  # Create new columns for 'nobasket_1' and 'nobasket_3' using the lagged values
  mutate(
    H_M2 = H_PM2,  # 1-point lag
    H_M3 = H_PM3,   # 3-point lag
    H_M4 = H_PM4   # 4-point lag
  ) %>%
  # Drop the temporary lagged columns
  select(-H_PM2, -H_PM3, -H_PM4)
```

```{r}
# Fill in N/A values for H_M2 with avg of H_M1 and H_M3, fill in N/A values for H_M3 with avg of H_M2 and H_M4


##TODO FIX BUGS IN THIS
df_enhanced <- df_enhanced %>%
  mutate(
    H_M2 = replace_na(H_M2, (H_M1 + H_M3) / 2),
    H_M3 = replace_na(H_M3, (H_M2 + H_M4) / 2)
  )
```

```{r}

# Add columns for each type of challenge

df_enhanced <- df_enhanced %>%
  mutate(
    oob_challenge = H_P - A_P,
    nofoul2_keepBall = H_P - A_FT2,
    nofoul2_jumpBall = (H_P+A_P)/2 - A_FT2,
    nofoul2_loseBall = A_P - A_FT2,
    nofoul3_keepBall = H_P - A_FT3,
    nofoul3_jumpBall = (H_P+A_P)/2 - A_FT3,
    nofoul3_loseBall = A_P - A_FT3,
    # lagged challenges below
    )
```

```{r}
df_filtered <- df_enhanced %>%
  filter(line == 0) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = gt,
    values_from = oob_challenge,
    id_cols = m,
  )
```


```{r}
# for writing a data.frame or list of data.frames to an xlsx file
write.xlsx(df_filtered, file='sample_data2.xlsx', sheetName='oob_challenge')
```
